content.problemStatement:
Chef recently finished a birthday cake, which can be represented by a grid with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM). For each valid iii, Chef placed a candle with height ai,ja_{i,j}ai,j​ in the cell in row iii and column jjj.

A rectangle with the top left corner in row r1r_1r1​ and column c1c_1c1​ and the bottom right corner in row r2r_2r2​ and column c2c_2c2​ (1≤r1<r2≤N1 \le r_1 \lt r_2 \le N1≤r1​<r2​≤N; 1≤c1<c2≤M1 \le c_1 \lt c_2 \le M1≤c1​<c2​≤M) is called sweet if the minimum of heights of candles on some two opposite corners is greater than the maximum of heights of candles on the other two opposite corners. Formally, one of the following conditions should hold:


min⁡(ar1,c1,ar2,c2)>max⁡(ar1,c2,ar2,c1)\min(a_{r_1,c_1}, a_{r_2,c_2}) \gt \max(a_{r_1,c_2}, a_{r_2,c_1})min(ar1​,c1​​,ar2​,c2​​)>max(ar1​,c2​​,ar2​,c1​​)
min⁡(ar2,c1,ar1,c2)>max⁡(ar1,c1,ar2,c2)\min(a_{r_2,c_1}, a_{r_1,c_2}) \gt \max(a_{r_1,c_1}, a_{r_2,c_2})min(ar2​,c1​​,ar1​,c2​​)>max(ar1​,c1​​,ar2​,c2​​)
The quality of the candle placement is defined as the number of sweet rectangles.

Find the quality of the initial cake and then process QQQ queries. In each query, we replace the candle currently placed in a row xxx and a column yyy by a new candle with height hhh. The queries are not independent, i.e. each such change affects all subsequent queries. After each query, find the quality of the current candle placement.

It is guaranteed that initially and after each query, the heights of the candles are pairwise distinct.
constraints: 0≤ai,j≤1090 \le a_{i,j} \le 10^90≤ai,j​≤109 for each valid iii and jjj
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers ai,1,ai,2,…,ai,Ma_{i,1}, a_{i,2}, \ldots, a_{i,M}ai,1​,ai,2​,…,ai,M​.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains three space-separated integers xxx, yyy and hhh describing a query.
content.outputFormat: First, print a line containing a single integer ― the quality of the initial placement. Then, for each query, print a line containing a single integer ― the quality of the placement after this query.
